channel.title: AI Firehose: What Is New
channel.description: We read the whole AI firehose so you do not have to, then distill it into one brief: what is new, what is breaking out, and what is worth your time, across the day, week, month, and quarter. You take what you want and leave the rest.

item.title: Every Level of Hermes Agent Explained
item.description: Jack Roberts: Jack Roberts breaks down a seven-level progression for building Hermes agents, from basic prompts to fully automated agentic operating systems. The video covers connecting multiple LLM providers (Claude, ChatGPT, Grok, DeepSeek) into unified workflows, building custom skills, orchestrating parallel sub-agents, and automating scheduled tasks.

item.title: Converting SQL Server types and rules to PostgreSQL
item.description: Google Cloud Tech: Google Cloud's Database Migration Service enables developers to convert SQL Server schemas and code to PostgreSQL equivalents using Gemini as an AI tutor. The service assists with type conversions like SQL Server user-defined types to PostgreSQL DOMAINs while explaining the migration process.

item.title: Microsoft's launched OpenClaw?
item.description: Jack Roberts: Microsoft Scout is an AI agent integrated into the Microsoft ecosystem that proactively acts on user behalf across Teams, email, and calendar without waiting for explicit prompts. It automates task setup and preparation like gathering meeting materials based on plain language work descriptions.

item.title: Coding with ADK to beat a text adventure game
item.description: Google Cloud Tech: Google Cloud Tech hosted a livestream demonstrating how to use the Agent Development Kit (ADK) to solve a text adventure game. Speakers Tilde Thurium and Abdel Sghiouar showed practical applications of agent development through a game-based example.

item.title: AI Fact or Fiction: The Fable Ban, Tokenmaxxing, Saaspocolypse — With Ara Kharazian
item.description: Alex Kantrowitz: Ara Kharazian, lead economist at Ramp, discusses data-driven analysis of AI spending patterns and whether businesses are getting real value from AI investments. The discussion evaluates claims about Anthropic overtaking OpenAI in adoption, "tokenmaxing" trends, DeepSeek's resurgence, and whether the predicted SaaS apocalypse is materializing.

item.title: I Tried the Open Source ElevenLabs Alternative (Voicebox)
item.description: Better Stack: Voicebox is a free open-source local AI voice studio for developers offering voice cloning, text-to-speech, system-wide dictation, and REST API access. The tool positions itself as a privacy-focused alternative to cloud services like ElevenLabs with unlimited generation capabilities.

item.title: Your Agent's Biggest Lie: "I Searched the Web" — Rafael Levi, Bright Data
item.description: AI Engineer: Agents claiming to search the web often fail silently by hitting CAPTCHAs, anti-bot systems, or falling back to stale training data while returning answers as if successful. A demonstration using Bright Data's Web MCP shows how real web access versus blocked access produces different results, revealing reliability issues in citations, real-time data, and hallucination rates.

item.title: How to Build a Business Using AI: From Idea to Launch in One Day
item.description: AI Master: YouTube video demonstrating Rocket 1.0, a platform designed to maintain context across fragmented AI workflows (research, notes, code, tracking). The video showcases building a startup MVP in one day by integrating research via Perplexity, planning in Claude, coding in Cursor, and tracking in Rocket, addressing the loss of context between disconnected tools.

item.title: AI agents can manage your passwords. Should we let them? Plus: The biggest Patch Tuesday ever.
item.description: IBM Technology: A podcast explores whether AI agents should be trusted to manage passwords and detect compromises, discussing both the promise of improved cybersecurity hygiene and potential pitfalls. The episode also covers Microsoft's June 2026 Patch Tuesday with over 200 CVEs and industry trends toward accepting greater cyber risk for innovation speed.

item.title: Minimax Code + M3 : Crazy Alternative to Claude,Codex!
item.description: AICodeKing: MiniMax M3 is an open-weights AI model designed for coding agents, long-context workflows, and multimodality with support for up to 1-million-token context windows. It provides API endpoints compatible with both Anthropic and OpenAI standards and integrates with coding tools like Cursor and Cline. MiniMax Code adds agent team collaboration features for implementation and verification tasks.

item.title: POV: A new feature enters the chat. 🕺✨
item.description: Google for Developers: Google for Developers posted a short, comedic video expressing empathy for software engineers dealing with new feature implementations. The video features speakers Justine Lai, M.E Francis, and Pete King and uses light-hearted humor to connect with developer audience sentiment.

item.title: NetNewsWire Status
item.description: Simon Willison: Simon Willison highlights NetNewsWire, an RSS reader first released in 2002 and open-sourced in 2018. He praises Brent Simmons's retirement project approach to making the software exceptionally good without commercial pressure.

item.title: New #1 open-source AI model is here!
item.description: AI Search: GLM 5.2 is presented as a leading open-source AI model with capabilities in digital rendering, animation, 3D modeling, code integration, and mathematical visualization. The video demonstrates its integration with Claude code and comparison of specifications and benchmarks.

item.title: AP Exclusive: Nvidia’s Jensen Huang says society needs ‘new social norms’ in the age of AI - AP News
item.description: Google News AI: Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang argues that society needs to establish new social norms in response to the advancement of AI technology. This reflects broader industry leadership perspectives on managing AI's societal integration.

item.title: A Functional Taxonomy of World Models
item.description: doppp: A functional taxonomy provides a structured classification of world models. This framework organizes approaches to modeling how agents learn and represent environment dynamics. The taxonomy serves as a conceptual tool for understanding and comparing different world modeling paradigms.

item.title: Anthropic's latest feud with the admin may help it, sales data suggests
item.description: pseudolus: Anthropic's public dispute with regulatory administration may have positive market effects according to sales data. The title suggests potential business benefits from the confrontation.

item.title: France to ditch Palantir's AI data tools in favour of domestic provider
item.description: devonnull: France is moving away from Palantir's AI data tools toward a domestic alternative provider. This reflects a strategic decision to reduce reliance on foreign commercial AI infrastructure.

item.title: Wolfram Language and Mathematica Version 15, AI Assistant, Symbolic Music, More
item.description: alok-g: Wolfram released Mathematica Version 15 featuring updates to Wolfram Language including an integrated AI Assistant and new symbolic music capabilities. The update expands the platform's AI and symbolic computation features.

item.title: Fable 5 Replacement Just Dropped: Fusion (Fable Level AI)
item.description: AI Revolution: OpenRouter introduced Fusion, a system that routes a single prompt to multiple AI models, compares their responses, identifies gaps, and synthesizes them into a stronger unified response. A budget Fusion setup achieved near-Fable 5 performance on deep research benchmarks at approximately half the cost.

item.title: Building AI Agents for AR Glasses and XR Devices with NVIDIA XR AI
item.description: NVIDIA Developer: NVIDIA has released NVIDIA XR AI, a framework designed to help developers build AI agents for AR glasses and XR wearable devices. The tool addresses the infrastructure gap between hardware readiness and the complexity of integrating live AI experiences into wearable applications.

item.title: Unlocking UK house-building with AI-accelerated planning
item.description: Google DeepMind: Google DeepMind partnered with UK government to develop an AI-powered prototype system for accelerating housing planning decisions. The system aims to speed up the approval process for house-building applications.

item.title: GitHub Models is no longer available to new customers
item.description: herrkanin: GitHub Models is no longer available to new customers. The service has been discontinued for new sign-ups, affecting access to the model hosting platform.

item.title: Agent Toolkit for AWS Explained in 2 Minutes
item.description: AWS Developers: Agent Toolkit for AWS equips AI coding agents with up-to-date documentation, best practices, and tested procedures for AWS services. It improves success rates and reduces wasted effort by supplementing agents' general knowledge with current information and guardrails.

item.title: The Briefing: Financial Services
item.description: Claude: Anthropic held a virtual briefing session where leaders from major banks discussed their deployments and use cases of Claude across their financial services organizations. The event showcased real-world applications of the Claude model in banking.

item.title: Claude Fable 5 with Harness Engineering & Loops Will Do Everything.
item.description: Cyril Imhof: Cyril Imhof covers Claude Fable 5 and discusses harness engineering, AI loops, and their applications in regulated industries. The video explores tensions between open and closed source AI models, risks of model shutdowns, and the state of AI-powered SaaS, drawing on experience since 2023 in the space.

item.title: Build Your Own Transaction Foundation Model for Financial Intelligence
item.description: NVIDIA Developer: NVIDIA Developer blog post on constructing transaction-based foundation models for financial analysis. Foundation models trained on transaction data can extract behavioral patterns from financial networks for intelligence applications.

item.title: SpaceX valuation balloons to $2.6T, briefly passes Amazon
item.description: TechCrunch AI: SpaceX's valuation reached $2.6 trillion, briefly surpassing Amazon's market value. The company's valuation increased by $1 trillion since secondary share trading began on Friday.

item.title: DeepSeek V4 Pro at 5% the cost of Claude – what it takes to close the gap
item.description: coolwulf: The post discusses DeepSeek V4 Pro's pricing advantage at 5% the cost of Claude, exploring what technical or operational factors enable this dramatic cost reduction and what gaps remain in capability or performance.

item.title: AI is potentially a Dunning-Kruger effect amplifier
item.description: binyu: The author argues that AI systems may amplify the Dunning-Kruger effect, where people with low competence overestimate their abilities. By making it easier to produce outputs that seem credible without deep understanding, AI could exacerbate this cognitive bias at scale.

item.title: Running Claude Code on Amazon Bedrock
item.description: AWS Developers: AWS Developers published a walkthrough on running Claude Code agentic tool via Amazon Bedrock with data residency within AWS infrastructure. The video covers environment setup, cross-region inference, prompt caching, the Bedrock Mantle endpoint, cost tracking per team, and Bedrock Guardrails for content governance.

item.title: Show HN: VoiceDraw – Talk system design out loud, the diagrams draw themselves
item.description: ajaypanthagani: VoiceDraw is a tool that automatically generates system design diagrams from spoken discussion. Users describe their architecture aloud and the system draws diagrams while capturing reasoning, open questions, and tradeoffs.

item.title: Leviathan Waking – On Anthropic/USG, and a new era in AI governance
item.description: speckx: Speckx presents an opinion on Anthropic's relationship with the US government and what it signals for AI governance. The piece suggests a shift toward closer government-industry coordination in AI policy.

item.title: Building long-running AI agents with ADK
item.description: Google Cloud Tech: Google Cloud Tech demonstrates the Agent Development Kit (ADK), an open source framework for building long-running, stateless, event-driven AI agents. The tutorial shows how a coordinator agent uses multi-agent delegation to route tasks to specialized sub-agents while maintaining clean reasoning chains.

item.title: Show HN: Ctx, save tokens by loading only the relevant tools
item.description: stevesolun: Ctx is a tool that automatically selects relevant skills, agents, MCP servers, and harnesses based on repository and task context, reducing token costs by limiting what gets loaded into the model's context. It works upstream by filtering tools before they are used, complementing downstream token-reduction approaches like rtk and ponytail.

item.title: Better Free AI Image than Midjourney?
item.description: Jack Roberts: Jack Roberts claims Ideogram 4.0 excels at rendering text within images, surpassing Midjourney and Google's Nano Banana Pro on design benchmarks, and offers this capability at no cost. The video positions Ideogram 4.0 as a free alternative for generating logos, signs, and infographics with accurate text rendering.

item.title: Android 17 launches with new multitasking tools as Google expands Gemini features
item.description: TechCrunch AI: Google released Android 17 and Wear OS 7 with new multitasking features, parental controls, and security tools. The launch includes a Pixel Drop that brings Google's latest AI models to devices, expanding Gemini feature availability.

item.title: Future Dynamic 3D Reconstruction: A 3D World Model with Disentangled Ego-Motion
item.description: Nils Morbitzer, Jonathan Evers, Artem Savkin: FR3D is a world model that predicts persistent 3D latent representations for future dynamic 3D reconstruction by explicitly decoupling scene evolution from agent trajectory. It uses teacher-student distillation with foundation models for robust zero-shot generalization and maintains geometric consistency over long prediction horizons.

item.title: Unified Multimodal Autoregressive Modeling with Shared Context-Visual Tokenizer is Key to Unification
item.description: Wujian Peng, Lingchen Meng, Yuxuan Cai: UniAR is a unified autoregressive framework that uses a single discrete visual tokenizer for both image understanding and generation. It employs multi-level feature fusion, bitwise quantization, parallel-bitwise-prediction, and a diffusion-based visual decoder to achieve state-of-the-art performance on image generation and editing while remaining competitive on multimodal understanding benchmarks.

item.title: Visual Verification Enables Inference-time Steering and Autonomous Policy Improvement
item.description: Mingtong Zhang, Dhruv Shah: VERITAS is a generator-verifier framework that pairs a pre-trained generalist robot policy with a gradient-free visual verifier to enable inference-time policy steering and self-improvement without additional training. Verified rollouts provide supervision for offline policy improvement, achieving comparable efficiency to expert demonstrations.

item.title: Variable-Width Transformers
item.description: Zhaofeng Wu, Oliver Sieberling, Shawn Tan: Variable-Width Transformers use an X-shaped architecture with wider early and late layers and narrower middle layers, employing parameter-free residual resizing. This nonuniform capacity allocation reduces FLOPs by 22 percent and KV cache memory by 15 percent while outperforming uniform-width baselines at matched parameter counts across dense and MoE models from 200M to 3B parameters.

item.title: MOCHI: Motion Enhancement of Collaborative Human-object Interactions
item.description: Jiye Lee, Yonghun Choi, Jungdam Won: MOCHI is a two-stage framework for enhancing noisy multi-human object interaction data by first optimizing hand grasps through physics constraints, then refining full-body motion using diffusion-based optimization with single-person motion priors. The system addresses contact misalignment, motion jitter, and incomplete articulation in collaborative human-object interaction captures.

item.title: EventDrive: Event Cameras for Vision-Language Driving Intelligence
item.description: Dongyue Lu, Rong Li, Ao Liang: EventDrive is a large-scale benchmark and model suite that integrates event camera streams, RGB frames, and language supervision for autonomous driving across perception, understanding, prediction, and planning tasks. EventDrive-VLM uses a multi-horizon event pyramid and temporal-horizon mixture-of-experts to fuse asynchronous event and frame-based data for driving reasoning and decision-making.

item.title: ReproRepo: Scaling Reproducibility Audits with GitHub Repository Issues
item.description: Shanda Li, Qiuhong Anna Wei, Jingwu Tang: ReproRepo is a scalable framework for reproducibility evaluation that uses GitHub issues as natural supervision to identify real-world reproduction blockers. The authors evaluated four LLM agent configurations on 1,149 machine learning papers and found that Codex with GPT-4 could identify semantically related blockers for approximately 90% of papers without executing code.

item.title: Sign-Rank, Index, and List Replicability: Connections and Separations
item.description: Ari Blondal, Hamed Hatami, Pooya Hatami: Blondal et al. establish connections and separations between sign-rank, Z2-index, and list replicability in learning theory. They show Z2-index is bounded by list replicability and resolve open questions about separations between these measures. They prove composition results relating list replicability to combinatorial measures like height and minimum star number.

item.title: EvolveNav: Proactive Preflection and Self-Evolving Memory for Zero-Shot Object Goal Navigation
item.description: Qi Chai, Wenhao Shen, Nanjie Yao: EvolveNav is a zero-shot object-goal navigation framework that enables test-time adaptation by building an agentic rule memory from past trajectories and using upper confidence bound-based retrieval to select effective navigation rules. It incorporates a memory-guided preflection module that forecasts action outcomes before execution, reducing inefficient exploration and achieving 10.1% improvement over baselines.

item.title: Adaptive Volumetric Mechanical Property Fields Invariant to Resolution
item.description: Rishit Dagli, Donglai Xiang, Vismay Modi: AdaVoMP predicts dense spatially-varying mechanical properties (Young's modulus, Poisson's ratio, density) for 3D objects. It uses a sparse adaptive voxel structure and a sparse transformer encoder-decoder that generates a unique representation autoregressively for each input shape, achieving 16x higher resolution than prior work.

item.title: Machiavelli is the most misunderstood thinker of all time – Ada Palmer
item.description: Dwarkesh Patel: Ada Palmer discusses how Machiavelli is widely misunderstood, examining his career as a Florentine diplomat and his motivations for writing "The Prince" after exile. She draws parallels between Machiavelli's decision to restrict access to his political insights and Szilard's nuclear warning to FDR, arguing both saw their knowledge as posing existential dangers if misused.

item.title: GPT‑NL: a sovereign language model for the Netherlands
item.description: root-parent: GPT-NL is a language model developed for the Netherlands with a focus on sovereignty. The model serves as a Dutch-specific alternative for language model capabilities in the Dutch language and context.

item.title: Learning Red Agent Policy from Observations for Neurosymbolic Autonomous Cyber Agents
item.description: Ankita Samaddar, Sandeep Neema, Daniel Balasubramanian: The work presents a policy learning technique using imitation learning to predict cyber-attacker actions from network observations in partially observable environments. The method integrates with neurosymbolic cyber-defense agents using behavior trees with learning-enabled components to achieve high prediction accuracy across diverse attack scenarios.

item.title: Darshana Graph: A Parallel Commentary Corpus for Comparative Indian Philosophy, with Stylometric and Exploratory Graph Analyses
item.description: Joy Bose: Darshana Graph is a corpus of over 125,000 philosophical text records from Hindu, Buddhist, and Jain traditions, including a structurally unique subset of 8,500 records with aligned commentary across eighteen historical interpreters. The authors present stylometric analysis methods and a large language model pipeline for extracting typed philosophical relationships, releasing the full corpus and code publicly.
